title: Market Brief — Saturday, March 28, 2026
summary: B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the sharpest single-day GPU move in the dataset, while availability sits at just 29%. TCO-adjusted B200 cost hits $11.49/hr, making next-gen inference economics underwater at medium and high pricing tiers — the Blackwell premium is real and unsustainable at current spot.
paragraphs: B200 spot surged 17.6% overnight to $9.19/hr, the sharpest single-day GPU move in the dataset, while availability sits at just 29%. TCO-adjusted B200 cost hits $11.49/hr, making next-gen inference economics underwater at medium and high pricing tiers — the Blackwell premium is real and unsustainable at current spot.

DeepSeek V3.2 sells output at $0.38/M against a TCO-adjusted Llama 70B production cost north of $5/M on H100s — DeepSeek is burning cash at roughly $4.50/M tokens to hold market share. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output commands 97% Ultra-tier margins on H200s, effectively subsidizing the entire industry price war from the top end while Anthropic holds a comfortable middle with Sonnet 4.6 at $15/M output earning Strong margins.

B200 spot is overvalued at $9.19/hr given 29% availability signals incoming supply relief and TCO-adjusted costs that destroy inference margins below premium tier. DeepSeek and Meta's sub-$1/M output models are undervalued for buyers — lock in enterprise contracts now before the subsidy window closes.
bullets: 17.6% overnight B200 spot increase is the largest single-day GPU price move tracked this quarter.
$11.49/hr TCO-adjusted B200 cost produces a negative 12% margin even at high-tier pricing.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a loss of approximately $4.50 per million tokens on H100 inference.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch three years ago.
$80/M output on o3 Pro delivers 97% margin, making it OpenAI's most profitable product by far.
24% GPU availability across both H100 and H200 pools signals tight but stable supply conditions.
